textByHash.180ea105ef5e91cf8e29fa2474f0fc96cda6b6b5: Of course. He's always happy to talk to possible business partners.
textByHash.29ce653cdafa791979b969ee8901c4e59884f276: Not very much, at the moment. I came from further south to set up a smithy here...
textByHash.6654385c3eb008909eb8c4e1a5f3570938536ce4: He said we'd have lots of customers once we set up our shops, and all he asks for is a cut of the profits.
textByHash.679bed21de423180f127dfc3c3fe80252138f1c4: They're just worried that they'll lose money when we open our shops.
textByHash.6d5da6bc3402fbf91535f72b6998c9694831cb1e: Yes, he's the one who persuaded us to come here.
textByHash.8794849d0bbdf9370d472a06eaf3a0dfeec05be9: ...but we still haven't set up the shops, so we have no customers yet.
textByHash.9720e7d2aae019ffb7b93989773706a4e595c06a: Don't ask me, I'm not from around here.
textByHash.b33d4d22d7618709f644e97dec9c2fb79d9caee5: You seem rather cheerful. Is there anything you're after?
textByHash.cdcd80ba75f0371a5ee3d4c65eb65314421b737f: Well, it's hot and full of sand.
textByHash.ea0684e4105e8fe9a33eadfb595755628497c539: Don't pay any attention to those people.
textByHash.ef1f8596fa4e2effefd8950cd8ab4f75261180fe: A town to the south of the Shantay Pass, called Pollnivneach.
